title: machine/applehv: Sync vfkit cpu/memory on start
body:
We end up serializing the base VM config and the vfkit JSON into one object.  This causes `podman machine set --memory X` to have no effect.  Fix this by synchronizing when deserializing.

A better fix would likely be to stop serializing this at all but that seems like a more invasive fix.



```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cgwalters* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [rhatdan](https://github.com/rhatdan) for approval. For more information see [the Kubernetes Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
This seemingly worked for me but now I'm confused because vfkit has memory apparently in units of bytes, yet we aren't doing that here, and somehow it works...digging

> This seemingly worked for me but now I'm confused because vfkit has memory apparently in units of bytes, yet we aren't doing that here, and somehow it works...digging

OK I finally figured this out.  I had originally thought that vfkit's Go code ended up serializing the JSON and passing it to the vfkit binary, but actually what is going on is vfkit is doing this:

```
	if vm.MemoryBytes != 0 {
		args = append(args, "--memory", strconv.FormatUint(vm.MemoryBytes, 10))
	}
```

And it's there that we're papering over this bytes/MiB confusion, because the vfkit command line is expecting MiB.

So...I think this PR is correct as is, but vfkit needs to clean this up.
LGTM. Restarting one failed test (probable flake)

That said I think the cleaner fix is for us to stop serializing the vfkit JSON at all, and synthesize it every `podman machine start`.  Another way to say this is, let's say tomorrow we add a change to podman to add a new device (say a virtualized GPU device) into the vfkit config.  Today, getting that change requires reprovisioning the machine; but there's no good reason for that instead of just a `podman machine stop+start`.

I was going for a small tactical change, but if there's not a reason for us to serialize it today, then I can look at changing per above (but probably after this lands).

> That said I think the cleaner fix is for us to stop serializing the vfkit JSON at all, and synthesize it every `podman machine start`. Another way to say this is, let's say tomorrow we add a change to podman to add a new device (say a virtualized GPU device) into the vfkit config. this is how podman 5 is wired. i dont think it will be a problem there.

PR needs rebase.
